reports.title: The Ceasefire That Depends on the Blockade
reports.subtitle: Iran and the US are negotiating peace while simultaneously threatening the conditions that make peace impossible.

reports.title: The IMF Abandoned Its Baseline
reports.subtitle: When the world's economic referee stops issuing forecasts and starts issuing warnings, that is the forecast.

reports.title: Ten Republicans Voted Against Trump on Immigration. It Will Not Matter.
reports.subtitle: The House passed a bill protecting Haitian immigrants. The Senate will kill it. Trump will veto anything that survives. The vote was not for policy; it was for the record.

reports.title: Parliament Voted Down the Ban. Starmer Called Tech CEOs Into Downing Street Anyway.
reports.subtitle: The UK government rejected an immediate social media ban for under-16s while simultaneously signaling it might do exactly that.

reports.title: Canada Wants to Label AI Content. The Problem Is Everything Is AI Content Now.
reports.subtitle: A House of Commons committee wants mandatory labels on AI-generated material. The recommendation is reasonable. The implementation problem is that the boundary between AI-assisted and AI-generated has already dissolved.

reports.title: Congress Is Trying to Lock China Out of the Chip Industry. Nvidia Says That's the Wrong Move.
reports.subtitle: The MATCH Act would block China from buying the machines that make advanced chips. Jensen Huang says restricting China just makes it build faster.

reports.title: Justice Jackson Says the Supreme Court Is Rubber-Stamping Illegal Policies. She Is Probably Right.
reports.subtitle: A sitting justice went to Yale Law School to accuse her colleagues of issuing 'scratch-paper musings' that let Trump implement policies courts already found unlawful.

reports.title: Putin Wants to Talk. Ukraine Has Heard This Before.
reports.subtitle: Russia is proposing direct negotiations in Istanbul on May 15 with no preconditions. The last time that happened, Kyiv drafted a deal and then dropped it under Western pressure.

reports.title: Yuan Settlements Hit $1 Trillion a Day. The Petrodollar System Is Being Stress-Tested.
reports.subtitle: The Iran war locked 16 percent of global oil production behind the Strait of Hormuz. The biggest beneficiaries are Russia and China.

reports.title: Trump Is About to Make Banks the Immigration Police.
reports.subtitle: An executive order requiring banks to collect citizenship data from all customers would turn the financial system into a deportation tool.

reports.title: The White House Is Giving Civilian Agencies a Model the Pentagon Banned.
reports.subtitle: The OMB is routing Anthropic's Mythos to civilian federal agencies while the Defense Department's blacklisting of Anthropic remains in force. One government is doing two opposite things at once.

reports.title: FISA Section 702 Is About to Expire. Congress Passed a 10-Day Extension.
reports.subtitle: Twenty House Republicans killed the five-year reauthorization. The surveillance authority that underpins most US counterterrorism and foreign intelligence collection is now running on a patch.

reports.title: Someone Made $950 Million Betting on Oil Minutes Before the Iran Ceasefire.
reports.subtitle: The CFTC is investigating futures trades that were positioned perfectly ahead of each of Trump's Iran announcements. The pattern is too consistent to be coincidence.

reports.title: Israel and Lebanon Have a Ceasefire. Hezbollah Is Not Part of It.
reports.subtitle: The 10-day truce excludes the party doing most of the fighting. Israel has reserved the right to strike at any time. Hezbollah says occupied land gives Lebanon the right to resist. This is not a ceasefire. It is a pause with paperwork.

reports.title: Rural Farmers Bet on Solar. Trump Changed the Rules After the Money Was Spent.
reports.subtitle: The USDA froze the REAP clean energy grant program and rescinded its funding announcement. Farmers who installed solar panels under the old rules are now holding debt with no grant to offset it.

reports.title: Trump Says He'll Fire Powell Even After the Chair Term Ends. The Market Is Betting He Won't.
reports.subtitle: Trump threatened to remove Powell not just as chair but as a Fed governor. Polymarket says 8.5% chance he actually does it by June. The gap between the threat and the market reveals the real bet.

reports.title: The DOJ Is 0 for 5 on Voter Data. It Has 25 Cases Left.
reports.subtitle: Courts keep rejecting the administration's demand for voter rolls. That does not explain why 17 Republican-led states handed over the data anyway.

reports.title: The Government Is Refunding $127 Billion in Tariffs. It Plans to Collect Them Again by July.
reports.subtitle: The Supreme Court said IEEPA tariffs were illegal. CBP opens a refund portal on April 20. Treasury Secretary Bessent says replacement tariffs will be back at roughly the same levels by summer.

reports.title: After the Ceasefire, Trump Told NATO to Stay Away. The UK and France Launched Their Own Mission.
reports.subtitle: The Strait of Hormuz reopened. NATO offered help. Trump said no and called the alliance useless. Within hours, Starmer and Macron announced a multinational shipping protection mission without the US.

reports.title: Sanders and the UAW Say AI Will Do to Factory Workers What NAFTA Did. They Are Probably Right.
reports.subtitle: Bernie Sanders and UAW President Shawn Fain held a Capitol Hill press conference warning that AI could wipe out 600,000 Midwest manufacturing jobs. Nobody in Congress is drafting a response.
